content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

February 4, 1987


D. B. Langman
Campaign Pathways
1121 Kansas Avenue
Modesto, CA 95351

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our Advice No. I-87-022

Dear Mr. Langman:

     This is in response to your request for advice under the
Political Reform Act.1/ Since you do not seek specific advice
on your duties under the Act, we will consider your letter to be
a request for informal assistance.2/

QUESTION

     You have asked if the allocation and use of bus benches for
political candidates is subject to some form of "equal"
availability ruling.

CONCLUSION

     The question you have presented is not covered by the
provisions of the Political Reform Act.

ANALYSIS

     The general provisions of the Political Reform Act require
the disclosure of sources of campaign contributions received by
candidates and elected officials, the disclosure of their assets

_______________
1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise noted.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Administrative
Code Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are
to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Administrative Code.

2/ Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with
the immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice
(Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c)(3).)

D. B. Langman
February 4, 1987
Page Two


and income and, in appropriate circumstances, their disquali-
fication from acting in their official capacities to avoid
conflicts of interest. In addition, the Act regulates the
activities of lobbyists in order that improper influences will
not be directed at public officials.

     The question you have presented is not of the type covered
by the Act. We are not aware of any laws which govern the use
of bus benches for political advertisement. You might wish to
contact the Federal Communications Commission for possible leads
on this subject.

     Should you in the future have any questions concerning the
application of the Political Reform Act, please feel free to
contact me at (916) 322-5901.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Diane M. Griffiths
                                        General Counsel

                                        By: Jeanette E. Turvill
                                            Legal Assistant

DMG:JET:km

CP
CAMPAIGN PATHWAYS
1121 Kansas Avenue                                                     Telephone:
Modesto, CA 95351                                              (209) 577-2989

                                                                      1/12/87

State of California Fair
Political Practices Commission
PO Box 207
Sacramento, CA 95804

Dear Sir/Madam:

     I would like to find out if the allocation and use of Bus Bench's for
political candidates is subject to some form of "equal" availability ruling.
Example: 100 available bus bench's that can be rented to display candidate
information. Candidate "A" requests 60 leaving only 40 for other candidates
who later request bench's to rent.
Question: 1) Must the bus bench renting agent equally allocate those 100?
          2) May a candidate basically "tie up" a majority?
          3) Should/must the renting agent go ahead and sell/rent as much/
             many as a candidate is willing to pay for?

     Our feeling is that the use of bus bench's is not like radio/TV and thus
not subject to "equal" time restrictions. It would be like establishing an
"equal" number of pieces of printing a print shop could do for any one
candidate, or "equal" number of yard signs a candidate could erect.

     One more consideration: Assuming our feelings are correct, then would
the strict "equal allocation" by the renting agent be an infringement and
illegal limitation on a candidates opportunity to advertise?

     Your earliest possible response will be appreciated.


                                                     Very Truly Yours
                                                     
                                                     DB Langman

January 15, 1987


D. B. Langman
1121 Kansas Avenue
Modesto, CA 95351

Re: 87-022

Dear Mr. Langman:

Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform
Act was received on January 13, 1987, by the Fair Political
Practices Commission. If you have any questions about your
advice request, you may contact Jeanette E. Turvill in the
Legal Division, directly at (916) 322-5901.

We try to answer all advice requests promptly. Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions,
or more information is needed, you should expect a response
within 21 working days. You also should be aware that your
letter and our response are public records which may be
disclosed to the public upon receipt of a proper request for
disclosure.

Very truly yours,

Diane M. Griffiths
General Counsel

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660
